title: PRD Structure
definition: Standard sections: problem, goals, stories, requirements, metrics, risks.
example: Your PRD opens with interview evidence for the problem, lists 3 goals with success metrics, then user stories — engineering knows why they're building before reading requirements.
whenToUse: Handing off discovery to engineering for build.
interviewPrompts: Walk me through how you'd write a PRD for X.

title: User Stories
definition: As a [user], I want [action], so that [outcome] — capturing user value in buildable units.
example: As a team lead, I want to duplicate a project template, so that I can onboard new clients in under 10 minutes instead of rebuilding from scratch.

title: Acceptance Criteria
definition: Testable conditions that define when a user story is complete.
example: Given a saved template, when the user clicks 'Duplicate,' then a new project is created with all tasks copied and the user sees a confirmation within 2 seconds.
whenToUse: Every user story before development starts.
frameworkSteps: Cover happy path and edge cases
officeUse: Definition of done and QA test plans.
